entries.summary: Current UK-market refresh: Born VZ promoted; Ford, Smart and Cupra offers clarified.
details: Promoted the 2026 Cupra Born VZ to shortlist after official confirmation of £45,995 pricing, standard Sennheiser audio, improved mechanical controls, 388-mile maximum range and 26-minute charging.

Strengthened the Ford Explorer value case: selected Extended Range trims explicitly qualify for the £1,500 grant, which Ford says also applies through lease/company-car and salary-sacrifice schemes; current retail support is substantial.
Strengthened Smart #5 value evidence with 0% APR and up to £2,350 support on #5 model lines, while retaining subscription, software, phone and security red flags.
Identified Tavascan V2 at £44,495 with Sennheiser as the audio-within-budget alternative to the faster, more expensive VZ trims.
Confirmed ES90 Personal/Business Contract Hire availability, but current stock pricing reinforces rather than resolves the affordability problem.

Confirmed official UK IM5/IM6 wireless CarPlay/Android Auto, configurator and test-drive availability; both remain research-only because reliability, security, insurance and control ergonomics are still insufficiently proven.

entries.summary: Corrected pricing, safety and subscription evidence in the UK EV shortlist.
details: No vehicle changed status this week; Smart #5 Brabus remains the only shortlist car.

Corrected Smart #5 Brabus to the current official UK figures of 335 miles WLTP and 646 hp, and replaced the generic subscription warning with Smart's published £84-per-year Premium Connectivity Package.
Corrected Cupra Tavascan VZ pricing: VZ1 is £54,930 and Sennheiser-equipped VZ2 is £59,830, materially weakening its value score and creating a direct budget-versus-audio conflict.

Removed the incorrect missing-Euro-NCAP flag from MG IM5. Its 2025 five-star result is now recorded, but it remains research-only because software, intrusive ADAS, menu-heavy controls, support maturity and theft/security evidence are still insufficient.
Strengthened the Volvo EX30 recall note using the official UK recall record: 10,577 affected vehicles may require a 70% charge limit until battery modules are inspected and replaced.
Raised Ford Explorer's value score slightly because Ford is actively advertising grant support and retail promotions, while retaining a trim-eligibility caveat for the desired Extended Range version.
Kept Kia EV6 GT as benchmark only and Tesla excluded.

entries.summary: Refreshed UK-market evidence across every tracked vehicle and enforced budget, safety and evidence thresholds.
details: Moved Volvo EX30 from watchlist to research after UK battery-overheat campaign evidence and reported electrical/display failures compounded its already poor control-layout fit.

Moved Audi A6 Sportback e-tron from shortlist to watchlist because its £63k-plus starting price breaches the stated £55k purchase ceiling; it can return only if a real salary-sacrifice quote is compelling.
Moved Volvo ES90 from watchlist to research because it is well over budget and no confirmed Euro NCAP result was retained in the evidence reviewed.

Kept Smart #5 Brabus on the shortlist and strengthened its charging/range/quality evidence: about 336 miles WLTP, up to 400 kW DC, five-star Euro NCAP and positive comfort/material-quality road tests.

Confirmed clearer UK availability and pricing for MG IM5 and IM6. IM6 has a five-star Euro NCAP result, but both remain research-only because software, phone integration, control ergonomics, theft/security and support maturity are insufficiently proven.
Kept Ford Explorer, Cupra Tavascan and Cupra Born on the watchlist; all now carry explicit Euro NCAP and dealer/lease-readiness notes.
Kept Kia EV6 GT as benchmark only and Tesla excluded.
Added 15+ images for every vehicle currently represented in the dashboard, including the EV6 GT benchmark.
Kept manufacturer/CDN images where easily available, and used reputable public press/media or Commons reference images where official galleries were difficult to extract cleanly.
Updated the photo policy to reflect personal-use visual-reference handling rather than redistribution-safe image packaging.

entries.summary: First evidence-backed market refresh and dashboard scoring pass.
details: Promoted Audi A6 Sportback e-tron to shortlist because it is strong on range, charging, integrated dashboard, B&O audio option and premium fit.
Kept Smart #5 Brabus on shortlist because it strongly matches sound and performance, but flagged screen/control, phone integration, support and security as validation risks.
Moved Volvo EX30 from shortlist to watchlist because the central-screen/minimal-controls philosophy conflicts with dashboard and physical-control preferences despite excellent performance/value.
Added Volvo ES90 as a high-potential but expensive watchlist car: strong sound, power, range and charging; dashboard and cost need validation.
Kept MG IM5 and IM6 in research despite exceptional specs because quality, controls, phone integration, security and UK support remain insufficiently proven.
Added visible key facts and source summaries to the dashboard cards.

entries.summary: Moved from flat CSV-only tracking toward structured data plus static dashboard.
details: Added sound/audio as the highest-weighted criterion.
Clarified that high performance is desirable, not a penalty, provided the car has usable comfort modes.
Added dashboard/control ergonomics as an explicit criterion, including dislike of non-integrated tablet-style screens.
Added reliable phone integration as a critical criterion.
Added security/theft resistance as a first-class criterion.
Kept Tesla excluded while allowing Chinese-brand cars if quality evidence is strong.
